title: Bridge Essay: Music in the brain.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Music in the brain.** — Nat Rev Neurosci. PMID: 35352057. DOI: 10.1038/s41583-022-00578-5. Music is ubiquitous across human cultures - as asource of affective and pleasurable experience, moving us both physically and emotionally - and learning to play music shapes both brain structure and brain function. Music processing in the brain - namely, the perception of melody, harmony and rhythm - has traditionally been studied as an auditory phenomenon using passive listening paradigms.
